alt: The red-and-white striped Les Éclaireurs Lighthouse standing on a lichen-covered rocky islet in the Beagle Channel, with the snow-capped Andean peaks of Tierra del Fuego visible across the channel under a moody overcast Patagonian sky
description: Les Éclaireurs Lighthouse on its rocky islet in the Beagle Channel east of Ushuaia, Argentina. The distinctive red-and-white striped conical tower has become popularly known as the "Lighthouse at the End of the World" (though Jules Verne's original referred to a different lighthouse on Isla de los Estados, further east). Visible: the lighthouse keeper's entrance, the official site sign, lichen-covered rocks, and the snow-capped peaks of Tierra del Fuego in the distance.
title: Faro les eclaireurs
